title: ulex minor subsp. gallii
intro: Ulex minor subsp. gallii, also known as the Irish gorse, is a subspecies of the Ulex minor plant, a type of spiny shrub that is native to Ireland. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as a diuretic and to have anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ulex minor subsp. gallii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
